strengths: Built end-to-end ETL and data models
weaknesses: No mention of VA EHR or VISN/CDW experience
No explicit SAS or SSRS experience
Less than required federal/government program analyst tenure
improvement_bullets: Optimized relational database queries to clean and reconcile healthcare-adjacent testing data, ensuring high data quality and standardization across cross-functional departments.
Led independent analytical projects to design and manage the data life cycle, from initial data collection and design to final reporting, for a 2M+ row loan portfolio.
Applied statistical methods, including regression and trend analysis, to perform predictive modeling on complex datasets, enabling data-driven population management strategies.
Collaborated with clinical and administrative stakeholders to translate complex requirements into technical workflows, improving data standardization and interoperability across legacy systems.
Developed automated Python-based scripts to facilitate the data life cycle, including standardized collection, cleaning, and validation of information to support enterprise-level reporting.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Program Analyst (Informatics) opportunity with the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs. I am interested in this opportunity because I am deeply committed to leveraging data-driven insights to improve the quality, efficiency, and effectiveness of healthcare delivery for those who have served our country. What interests me about working for the VA is its mission to integrate clinical processes with advanced informatics to improve veteran health outcomes, which inspires me because of the potential for large-scale, life-changing impact. I believe my strong background in data architecture and my experience in cross-functional stakeholder communication would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached complex data challenges with a focus on process optimization and technical rigor. In fact, at Haven Technologies, I led a project to reconcile complex insurance data by optimizing relational database queries and integrating multiple API sources into a unified schema. This effort reduced project delivery delays by 25% and established a more reliable, clean data structure for engineering teams. Ultimately, I know that the Program Analyst (Informatics) role at the VA would allow me to bring my proficiency in SQL and Python-based ETL pipelines to the critical task of optimizing EHR-linked informatics tools. Additionally, my role as a Data Analyst & Operations Lead at Shine Masters Cleaning Services enabled me to develop my skills in translating complex performance data into actionable insights for executive leadership. In that capacity, I automated scheduling and intake workflows using Zapier and designed Power BI dashboards that replaced manual reporting, saving 8 hours per week and increasing departmental efficiency by 25%. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my background in data modeling and statistical analysis to support the VA’s clinical and administrative healthcare data initiatives. I know that my technical expertise and commitment to workflow improvement align with what it takes to succeed in this role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
